spec_match.rationale: The demo uses hardcoded JavaScript to simulate Chrome 150's behavior on mock elements, rather than letting the browser natively demonstrate the filter rules on real sandboxed and same-origin iframes.

interactive.rationale: While the select dropdown is interactive, the visual result is a synthetic simulation driven by style.filter = 'none' rather than real-world iframe rendering.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The explanation of SVG filters being stripped from sandboxed frames matches the feature's core definition.

edge_cases.rationale: It simulates CSS shorthand vs SVG displacement filters but lacks live edge case verification with real sandboxed documents.

references.rationale: The page does not include the required chromestatus.com/feature/5117170452398080 reference link.

design_system.rationale: Follows styling guidelines well with clean contrast, responsive layout, and standard crumbs/lede blocks.

openQuestions.title: Add missing chromestatus.com reference link
openQuestions.detail: Every concept page must include a link to the associated ChromeStatus entry. Add a link to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5117170452398080 in the references section or crumbs block.

openQuestions.title: Convert mock elements to real iframes
openQuestions.detail: Instead of using simulated styled <div> layers (.sim-embed) and forcing filter = 'none' via hardcoded JS, use real <iframe> and <iframe sandbox> elements. Apply the CSS/SVG filters to the parent wrapper divs and let the browser's native rendering engine demonstrate whether the filter is stripped or applied, creating a genuine spec-compliant capability demo.

summary: The Sandbox Filter Test uses a synthetic JS simulation on mock elements rather than leveraging actual sandboxed iframes to prove Chrome 150's native behavior. It also misses the required ChromeStatus link.
